highlights:

| Azure | Python | REST APIs | Docker | AWS Bedrock | Sage
...
AI Engineer at [Macy's](https://www.linkedin.com/company/macy)
...
Senior AI Engineer & Architect — 11+ years building production Gen AI, Agentic AI, and ML platforms. I design and ship enterprise-grade AI systems that actually run in production. Over 11+ years I've worked across investment banking (Goldman Sachs), healthcare (CVS Health), consumer tech (Apple), and retail (Nike) — building everything from multi-agent LangGraph workflows and RAG pipelines on Amazon Bedrock to HIPAA-compliant clinical ML platforms and on-device NLP. Core stack and expertise: - Generative AI & LLMs: GPT-4, Claude, LLaMA, Mistral, BERT, Transformers, Fine-Tuning, LoRA, QLoRA, PEFT, RLHF, Prompt Engineering, Embeddings, Guardrails - Agentic AI & Frameworks: LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, LangSmith, AutoGen, CrewAI, Promptfoo, MCP, multi-agent orchestration, tool calling, function calling - RAG & Vector Databases: Pinecone, FAISS, Chroma, Weaviate, Elasticsearch, semantic chunking, hybrid search, retrieval evaluation - ML & Deep Learning: PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras, XGBoost, LightGBM, Scikit-learn, ONNX, JAX, Ray, Hugging Face, CNNs, RNNs, LSTMs, spaCy, NLTK, OpenCV - Cloud AI Platforms: AWS (SageMaker, Bedrock, Lambda, EC2, S3, Kinesis, EKS, MSK, Glue, CloudWatch), GCP (Vertex AI, BigQuery), Azure (AI, ML, OpenAI), Databricks - MLOps & Model Serving: MLflow, Kubeflow, LLMOps, GenAIOps, Model Registry, Feature Store, Drift Detection, A/B Testing, FastAPI, vLLM, TorchServe, Triton Inference Server - Data & Streaming: Apache Kafka, Apache Spark, PySpark, Apache Airflow, Hadoop, Hive, ETL/ELT, distributed training, CUDA, GPU optimization - Programming: Python (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn), SQL, Scala, Java, R, C++, JavaScript - DevOps & Infra: Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, Jenkins, GitHub Actions, ArgoCD, Terraform, CloudFormation, CI/CD - Databases: PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Neo4j, Snowflake, DynamoDB, Redis - AI Safety & Compliance: HIPAA, SOX, GDPR, Responsible AI, bias detection, red-teaming, differential privacy, federated learning, SHAP, LIME, LLM-as-judge - Monitoring: LangSmith, Arize AI, Prometheus, Grafana, CloudWatch, Great Expectations A few results I'm proud of: - $12M+ in potential trading losses caught annually via custom PyTorch anomaly detection (Goldman Sachs) - 70% reduction in pharmacist lookup time with a clinical RAG system over 500K+ drug interaction docs (CVS Health) - 15% reduction in Siri speech recognition word error rate via Transformer optimization (Apple) - $4M annual overstock reduction from Nike Direct demand forecasting models

results.title:
Macy's E-commerce Platform Optimization Specialist (Remote)
 – Real Jobs
highlights:
Macy's E-commerce Platform Optimization Specialist (Remote) – Real Jobs
...
# Macy's E-commerce Platform Optimization Specialist (Remote)
...
🏢 Macy's 📍 Remote, DE 💼 🌐 Remote 💰 EUR 55,000 – 70,000 / year 🏭 General 📅 2026-06-16
...
### 🎯 ROLE OVERVIEW
...
As an E-commerce Platform Optimization Specialist, you will play a critical role in driving the performance and scalability of Macy's e-commerce platform. You will report directly to the Director of E-commerce Platform and work closely with cross-functional teams to identify areas of improvement, develop and implement optimization strategies, and analyze key performance indicators. This role has a significant impact on the customer experience, revenue growth, and overall business success.
...
. Develop and execute data-driven optimization strategies to improve e-commerce platform performance, scalability, and reliability.
...
. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify technical debt, implement fixes, and optimize system configurations.
...
. Analyze key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure platform performance, identify bottlenecks, and develop targeted solutions.
...
. Design and implement A/B testing and experimentation frameworks to inform optimization decisions.
...
. Develop and maintain comprehensive documentation of platform architecture, technical decisions, and optimization strategies.
...
. Provide technical guidance and support to internal stakeholders,
...
managers, and analysts.
...
. Stay up-to-date with emerging trends, technologies, and best practices in e-commerce platform optimization.
...
### ✅ REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS
...
. 5+ years of experience in e-commerce platform optimization, performance engineering, or a related field.
...
. Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
...
. Proven experience with cloud-based e-commerce platforms (e.g. Shopify, Magento), APIs, and microservices.
...
. Strong proficiency in programming languages such as Java, Python, or C#.
...
. Familiarity with DevOps tools, such as Docker, Kubernetes, and Jenkins.
...
. Experience with data analytics tools, such as Google Analytics, Tableau, or Power BI.
...
### ⭐ PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS
...
. Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
...
. Certifications in e-commerce platform optimization, performance engineering, or DevOps.
...
. Experience with e-commerce platform vendors, such as Amazon, Salesforce, or SAP.
...
. Familiarity with machine learning algorithms and data modeling techniques.
...
Our team is a collaborative and dynamic group of professionals who share a passion for e-commerce and digital innovation. We're a remote-first team, and we prioritize open communication, clear goals, and a supportive work environment. We've achieved significant success in recent projects, including the launch of our new e-commerce platform and the optimization of our payment processing system.
...
To apply, please submit your resume, cover letter, and a brief description of how you've driven e-commerce platform optimization in your previous roles. We'll review applications on a rolling basis and expect to conduct interviews within 2-3 weeks. Don't miss this opportunity to join our dynamic team and drive growth for Macy's!
...
### 📋 Job Details
...

...
Data Scientist at [Macy's](https://www.linkedin.com/company/macy)
...
As a results-driven Data Scientist, I bring 5 years of experience in developing and deploying machine learning models, deep learning architectures, and NLP solutions to support data-driven decision-making. I have strong expertise in Python, R, and SQL, leveraging tools like Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, and Keras to build scalable AI systems. My experience spans designing end-to-end machine learning pipelines on Azure Machine Learning and AWS SageMaker, ensuring efficient data ingestion, model training, and deployment. I am highly skilled in cloud platforms, including AWS (S3, Redshift, Lambda, Kinesis, Glue) and Azure (Blob Storage, Data Lake, Synapse, Event Hubs), and have worked extensively with big data technologies such as Spark, Hadoop, Hive, and Kafka. In addition to model development, I am proficient in data visualization tools like Power BI, Tableau, and Matplotlib, which I use to communicate key insights through dashboards and reports. My work often involves managing and querying databases, including PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, and NoSQL systems like MongoDB, Cassandra, and DynamoDB. I also specialize in real-time analytics using Kafka, Spark Streaming, and Azure Stream Analytics, and have deep expertise in building scalable ETL/ELT pipelines with Python, Airflow, AWS Glue, and Azure Data Factory. My knowledge extends to ML Ops, where I utilize Docker, Kubernetes, and Jenkins to automate deployments and streamline machine learning workflows. Beyond technical capabilities, I excel in natural language processing using tools like spaCy, NLTK, and BERT for text analytics tasks such as sentiment analysis and named entity recognition. I apply advanced ML techniques including transfer learning, ensemble methods, and hyperparameter tuning using tools like XGBoost, LightGBM, Grid Search, and AutoML. As an advocate for data security and governance, I implement RBAC and RLS in systems like PostgreSQL and Power BI. I thrive in Agile team environments, collaborating cross-functionally to deliver impactful AI-driven solutions, and routinely apply statistical analysis and A/B testing to refine strategies and boost business performance.
...
### Data Scientist at [Macy's](https://www.linkedin.com/company/macy) (Current)
...
As a Data Science and Machine Learning professional, I have developed and fine-tuned a wide range of ML and deep learning models using Python (Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Keras), focusing on hyperparameter tuning, feature engineering, and model generalization. I’ve handled NLP tasks using NLTK and spaCy, implementing sentiment analysis, named entity recognition, and tokenization. Leveraging PyTorch Lightning and TensorFlow, I built flexible, production-ready architectures for use cases like fraud detection, churn prediction, and recommendation systems. My experience includes designing scalable ML pipelines with Azure Machine Learning Studio, Azure Data Factory, and Databricks, as well as managing big data solutions through Spark, Hadoop, Hive, and Pig. I containerized ML models with Docker and developed CI/CD pipelines using Jenkins and GitHub Actions to automate training and deployment. I also created and optimized SQL queries, stored procedures, and ETL workflows to support real-time analytics and reporting. On the visualization and data governance front, I built dynamic dashboards using Power BI, Seaborn, and Matplotlib, integrating with APIs and cloud data sources. I ensured secure data handling by implementing RBAC in PostgreSQL and RLS in Power BI. My collaborative contributions included maintaining code repositories, managing pull requests, and performing code reviews to ensure best practices in model development and deployment. Show less

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/not-that-dave-ramsey_agenticai-claudecode-aistrategy-activity-7448005130240790528-ygUA
...
Skills are different.
...
skill isn't just
...
-specific reasoning, all
...
I've been building agentic AI systems for enterprise clients for two years. And I had a conversation recently that reframed something I'd been seeing but hadn't quite named.
...
AI world.
...
I realized: I don't actually care that much about technical skills anymore. Not for the roles that matter most right now. What I'm looking for — what I keep wishing clients had more of — is product thinking.
...
The ability to look at a tool and ask: what does this actually do for a user? Not what features does it have. Not what's technically impressive. What problem does it solve, completely and honestly?
...
Here's what nobody's saying out loud: every framework product leaders already live by — writing clear requirements, managing context across a team, thinking in outcomes not outputs, running a scrum — works just as well directing an LLM agent as it does a human team. (Scrum, it turns out, is basically just context management. LLMs have the same problem.)
...
The people who've spent careers thinking about what makes something genuinely valuable are going to have a much easier time directing AI than the people who've spent careers writing the code.
...
Am I wrong? I'd genuinely like to know what the engineers think. #AIStrategy #AgenticAI #DataScience
...
### https://www.linkedin.com/posts/not-that-dave-ramsey_claude-code-the-complete-guide-activity-7415117667629477888-YgZ_
...
Activity: Mark Leonard liked
...
Had a great time teaching multi-agent AI workflows today.
...
The big insight I keep coming back to: AI doesn't replace engineering fundamentals—it amplifies them.
...
If you have good code hygiene, intentional feature definitions, and clear product thinking, tools like Claude Code will 10x your output. If you don't, they'll 10x your chaos.
...
A few things that resonated with the group:
...
On the role shift happening right now:
...
Engineers are moving from syntax/debugging to product ownership. The question isn't "how do I write this code" anymore—it's "what outcome am I trying to deliver?"
...
On why most agent failures happen:
...
It's almost never the model. It's the inputs and context. Better prompts, preloaded context, and explicit exploration instructions fix 90% of "the AI isn't working" problems.
...
On orchestration being the secret sauce:
...
The magic isn't in having agents—it's in how you coordinate them. Parallel for research, sequential for development. Consultation patterns over simulating human teams. Markdown handoffs to preserve context.
...
On context management:
...
"Scrum is essentially a human-based process for context management." We've been solving this problem for decades. LLMs just made us think about it differently.
...
The tooling landscape is moving fast, but the fundamentals stay the same: clear intentions, good hygiene, measured iteration.
...
If you're getting started with Claude Code specifically, I wrote a practical guide that covers the patterns I teach: https://lnkd.in/gu8S9iPZ What patterns are working for your team?

## Job description
...
Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward.
...
As a Software Engineer on the Shopping Ads team, you will be an "AI First" engineer dedicated to solving complex issues using Generative AI . You will design, implement, and launch innovative Large Language Model (LLM) and Machine Learning (ML) systems to enhance the shopping journey for users worldwide.
...

...
* Write product or system development code. 
* Collaborate with peers and stakeholders through design and code reviews to ensure best practices amongst available technologies (e.g., style guidelines, checking code in, accuracy, testability, and efficiency).
...
* Execute and analyze A/B experiments to evaluate performance and ensure long-term value.
...
* Triage
...
and debug/track/resolve

...

* 2 years of experience programming in Python or C++.
* 1 year of experience with applied machine learning.
* 1 year of experience building or using large-scale, production quality Machine Learning (ML) systems.### Preferred qualifications:
...
* Master's degree or PhD in Computer Science, or a technical area related to Applied AI/ML engineering.
* 2 years of experience with data structures and algorithms.
* Experience developing accessible technologies.
